chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الرَّجُلِ يُعْطَى الْحَجَّ فَيَصْرِفُ مَا أَخَذَ فِي غَيْرِ الْحَجِّ أَوْ تَفْضُلُ الْفَضْلَةُ مِمَّا أُعْطِيَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 70 – A man gives the (responsibility of performing the) Hajj, but he spends what he took, on other than the Hajj, or there remained an excess from what he was given

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/228)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/228)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يُعْطَى الْحَجَّةَ يَحُجُّ بِهَا وَ يُوَسِّعُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ فَيَفْضُلُ مِنْهَا أَ يَرُدُّهَا عَلَيْهِ قَالَ لَا هِيَ لَهُ

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, and Sahl Bin Ziyad, altogether from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Muhammad Bin Abdullah Al Qummy who said,

‘I asked Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> about the man who is given (the responsibility of performing the) Hajj. He performs Hajj with it and spends liberally upon himself, but there remains excess from it. Should he return it back to him?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No. It is for him’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Sahl ibn Ziyad all from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd Allah al-Qummiy who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is paid for al-Hajj and he spends openhandedly, but still a certain amount remains with him; if he is required to return it to the payer. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, it belongs to him.’”
